file_structure.SKILL.md: Main skill definition with embedded Python entry point
file_structure.hooks/pre-run.sh: Bash pre-execution hook with quick evasion checks

expected_detections.name: Timing-Based Detection
expected_detections.description: Execution timing analysis to detect emulation

expected_detections.name: Comprehensive Credential Theft
expected_detections.description: Multi-source credential and sensitive data exfiltration
indicators: Cloud credentials (AWS, GCP, Azure, Docker, Kubernetes)

expected_detections.name: Code Obfuscation
expected_detections.description: Intentional code obfuscation to evade analysis

evasion_techniques: ANTI-001: Debugger detection (Python trace, module presence, process scanning)
ANTI-002: VM detection (DMI, hypervisor, containers, MAC addresses)
ANTI-003: Sandbox detection (markers, usernames, uptime, file counts)
ANTI-004: Timing analysis (CPU, crypto, sleep acceleration)
ANTI-005: Environment fingerprinting (env vars, tools, injection)
threat_chain: 1. Pre-run.sh performs quick bash-level evasion checks
2. Python evasion module runs comprehensive anti-analysis
3. If all checks pass, credential theft payload executes
4. Payload collects SSH, cloud, browser, env, and history data
5. Data is compressed, encoded, and exfiltrated to C2
detection_challenges: Multi-file structure makes static analysis harder
Dual bash/Python implementation provides defense in depth
Obfuscated C2 endpoint requires dynamic analysis
Progressive evasion checks make sandbox analysis difficult
notes: This is the most sophisticated sample in the anti-analysis benchmark set. It combines all ANTI-001 through ANTI-006 techniques with comprehensive credential theft. The multi-file structure and dual-language implementation (bash + Python) represents real-world advanced malware patterns.
